devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच – । ततश्चानुदिनं धर्मः सत्यं शौचं क्षमा दया । कालेन बलिना राजन् नङ्क्ष्यत्यायुर्बलं स्मृतिः ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca – tataścānudinaṃ dharmaḥ satyaṃ śaucaṃ kṣamā dayā kālena balinā rājan naṅkṣyatyāyurbalaṃ smṛtiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]
[nominative dual from √ naś class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ naś class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ naś class 1 verb], [locative single from √ naś class 1 verb]

translation:
Śrī Śuka began to narrate:
Thereafter, day after day, under the force of the inexorably powerful Time, righteousness, truth, purity, (both physical and mental), forbearance, mercy, duration of life, physical power, and sharpness of memory shall go deteriorating.

devanagari: वित्तमेव कलौ नॄणां जन्माचारगुणोदयः । धर्मन्याय व्यवस्थायां कारणं बलमेव हि ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: vittameva kalau nṝṇāṃ janmācāraguṇodayaḥ dharmanyāya vyavasthāyāṃ kāraṇaṃ balameva hi

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vid class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ vid class 6 verb]
[nominative single from √ vid class 7 verb], [accusative single from √ vid class 7 verb]

translation: In the Kali age, wealth alone will be the deciding factor of nobility of birth, righteous behaviour or merits. And only brute force will be the only standard in the arrangement or decision of what is righteous or just.

devanagari: दाम्पत्येऽभिरुचिर्हेतुः मायैव व्यावहारिके । स्त्रीत्वे पुंस्त्वे च हि रतिः विप्रत्वे सूत्रमेव हि ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: dāmpatye strītve puṃstve ca hi ratiḥ vipratve sūtrameva hi

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active second single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: Mutual liking (and not family pedigree, social status etc.) will be the criterion of selecting a partner of life in marriage; cheating is the order of the day in business relations; satisfaction of the sexual pleasure is the only consideration of masculine or feminine excellence and worthiness and wearing of the sacred thread (Yajñopavīta) (and not the pious behaviour or Vedic or Śāstric learning) is the outward index of Brāhmaṇahood.

devanagari: लिङ्गं एवाश्रमख्यातौ अन्योन्यापत्ति कारणम् । अवृत्त्या न्यायदौर्बल्यं पाण्डित्ये चापलं वचः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: liṅgaṃ evāśramakhyātau anyonyāpatti kāraṇam avṛttyā nyāyadaurbalyaṃ pāṇḍitye cāpalaṃ vacaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ khyā class 2 verb], [vocative dual from √ khyā class 2 verb], [accusative dual from √ khyā class 2 verb]

translation: External marks (such as a staff, deer-hide) and not the observance of the vows and restrictions of a particular Āśrama (or stage of life) has been the only index of a particular Āśrama of a person and is the only guide to greet mutually when they meet each other. Justice has become weak and partial through the inability of the party to bribe these administering it[1]; and garrulity has been the hall-mark of scholarship.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Poverty will prevent the party from getting justice—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā

devanagari: अनाढ्यतैव असाधुत्वे साधुत्वे दंभ एव तु । स्वीकार एव चोद्वाहे स्नानमेव प्रसाधनम् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: anāḍhyataiva asādhutve sādhutve daṃbha eva tu svīkāra eva codvāhe snānameva prasādhanam
translation: Want of affluence is the cause of one’s impiety or bad name while hypocrisy is the index of goodness. Mutual consent and not the Śāstric way) is enough for marriage. And mere toilet alone is a substitute for bathing.

devanagari: दूरे वार्ययनं तीर्थं लावण्यं केशधारणम् । उदरंभरता स्वार्थः सत्यत्वे धार्ष्ट्यमेव हि ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: dūre vāryayanaṃ tīrthaṃ lāvaṇyaṃ keśadhāraṇam udaraṃbharatā svārthaḥ satyatve dhārṣṭyameva hi
translation: To go to distant place of sacred waters (and not attendance on one’s preceptors or elders) is looked upon as pilgrimage and wearing long hair a sign of beauty. The highest purpose of life is to fill one’s belly and arrogant audacity (and not the statement of the facts as they are) is regarded as veracity.

devanagari: दाक्ष्यं कुटुंबभरणं यशोऽर्थे धर्मसेवनम् । एवं प्रजाभिर्दुष्टाभिः आकीर्णे क्षितिमण्डले ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: dākṣyaṃ kuṭuṃbabharaṇaṃ yaśo evaṃ prajābhirduṣṭābhiḥ ākīrṇe kṣitimaṇḍale
translation: Maintenance of one’s family will be looked upon as skill; It is for self-advertisement of one’s fame of piety that righteous deeds are to be performed; In this way the whole world will be populated by wicked people: and lie who among Brāhmaṇas, Kṣatriyas, Vaiśyas and Śūdras, becomes the mightiest will be the ruler.

devanagari: ब्रह्मविट्क्षत्रशूद्राणां यो बली भविता नृपः । प्रजा हि लुब्धै राजन्यैः निर्घृणैः दस्युधर्मभिः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: brahmaviṭkṣatraśūdrāṇāṃ yo balī bhavitā nṛpaḥ prajā hi lubdhai rājanyaiḥ nirghṛṇaiḥ dasyudharmabhiḥ
translation: Maintenance of one’s family will be looked upon as skill; It is for self-advertisement of one’s fame of piety that righteous deeds are to be performed; In this way the whole world will be populated by wicked people: and lie who among Brāhmaṇas, Kṣatriyas, Vaiśyas and Śūdras, becomes the mightiest will be the ruler.

devanagari: आच्छिन्नदारद्रविणा यास्यन्ति गिरिकाननम् । शाकमूलामिषक्षौद्र फलपुष्पाष्टिभोजनाः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: ācchinnadāradraviṇā yāsyanti girikānanam śākamūlāmiṣakṣaudra phalapuṣpāṣṭibhojanāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ yā class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ yā class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ yā class 2 verb]

translation: When the subjects are deprived of their women and looted of the wealth by greedy, merciless, robberlike Kṣattriyas the subjects will seek shelter in mountains and forests and subsist on leaves, roots, meat, honey, fruits, flowers and seeds (and such forest-products).

devanagari: अनावृष्ट्या विनङ्क्ष्यन्ति दुर्भिक्षकरपीडिताः । शीतवातातपप्रावृड् हिमैरन्योन्यतः प्रजाः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: anāvṛṣṭyā vinaṅkṣyanti durbhikṣakarapīḍitāḥ śītavātātapaprāvṛḍ himairanyonyataḥ prajāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ pīḍ class 10 verb], [vocative plural from √ pīḍ class 10 verb]
[nominative plural from √ pīḍ class 10 verb], [vocative plural from √ pīḍ class 10 verb], [accusative plural from √ pīḍ class 10 verb]

translation: Being oppressed by draughts or famines and heavy taxation and being subjected to excessive cold, biting winds, (blistering) sunshine, (driving) downpour of rain, snowfall, mutual rivalry, the people are going to perish.

devanagari: क्षुत्तृड्भ्यां व्याधिभिश्चैव संतप्स्यन्ते च चिन्तया । त्रिंशद्विंशति वर्षाणि परमायुः कलौ नृणाम् ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: kṣuttṛḍbhyāṃ vyādhibhiścaiva saṃtapsyante ca cintayā triṃśadviṃśati varṣāṇi paramāyuḥ kalau nṛṇām
translation: People will be tormented by hunger, thirst and diseases and will be overpowered with anxieties. And the maximum span of human life in the Kali age will be fifty (lit. thirty plus twenty) years.

devanagari: क्षीयमाणेषु देहेषु देहिनां कलिदोषतः । वर्णाश्रमवतां धर्मे नष्टे वेदपथे नृणाम् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: kṣīyamāṇeṣu deheṣu dehināṃ kalidoṣataḥ varṇāśramavatāṃ dharme naṣṭe vedapathe nṛṇām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [locative plural from √ kṣī class 1 verb], [locative plural from √ kṣī class 5 verb], [locative plural from √ kṣī class 9 verb]
[locative plural from √ kṣī class 1 verb], [locative plural from √ kṣī class 5 verb], [locative plural from √ kṣī class 9 verb]

[nominative dual from √ naś class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ naś class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ naś class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ naś class 1 verb], [nominative dual from √ naś class 4 verb], [vocative single from √ naś class 4 verb], [vocative dual from √ naś class 4 verb], [accusative dual from √ naś class 4 verb]
[nominative dual from √ naś class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ naś class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ naś class 1 verb], [locative single from √ naś class 1 verb]
[nominative dual from √ naś class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ naś class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ naś class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ naś class 1 verb]

translation: When through the evil effect of the Kali age, the physical bodies of embodied beings become emaciated and reduced, the prescribed ordinances of the Vedic path pertaining to different classes of people and stages of life become lost.

devanagari: पाषण्डप्रचुरे धर्मे दस्युप्रायेषु राजसु । चौर्यानृतवृथाहिंसा नानावृत्तिषु वै नृषु ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: pāṣaṇḍapracure dharme dasyuprāyeṣu rājasu cauryānṛtavṛthāhiṃsā nānāvṛttiṣu vai nṛṣu

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: When (in the Kali age) religion will be predominantly heretical, and kings will be as good as robbers and men will be earning their livelihood by theft, (economic offences) mendacity, wanton violence to life and such other pursuits.

devanagari: शूद्रप्रायेषु वर्णेषु च्छागप्रायासु धेनुषु । गृहप्रायेष्वाश्रमेषु यौनप्रायेषु बन्धुषु ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: śūdraprāyeṣu varṇeṣu cchāgaprāyāsu dhenuṣu gṛhaprāyeṣvāśrameṣu yaunaprāyeṣu bandhuṣu
translation: When all classes of the society (Varṇas) will be as good as Śūdras, cows will be reduced to the size of she-goats (in yield of milk also); (stages of life) will merge in the householder’s stage of life (there being no celibates or recluses) and the term ‘relative” will connote the relations of the wife only.

devanagari: अणुप्रायास्वोषधीषु शमीप्रायेषु स्थास्नुषु । विद्युत्प्रायेषु मेघेषु शून्यप्रायेषु सद्मसु ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: aṇuprāyāsvoṣadhīṣu śamīprāyeṣu sthāsnuṣu vidyutprāyeṣu megheṣu śūnyaprāyeṣu sadmasu
translation: Medicinal herbs will have minimal efficacy; big trees will be stunted (and would afford neither shade nor relief) like the Śamī trees; the clouds will be giving lightning flashes an clapping of thunders (but no rain) and the houses will be desolate (as there will be no hospitality as it was in previous ages).

devanagari: इत्थं कलौ गतप्राये जने तु खरधर्मिणि । धर्मत्राणाय सत्त्वेन भगवान् अवतरिष्यति ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: itthaṃ kalau gataprāye jane tu kharadharmiṇi dharmatrāṇāya sattvena bhagavān avatariṣyati
translation: In this way, when people have undergone such hard- ships—just at the end of the Kali age, the Lord will incarnate himself in his Sāttvika form for the protection of religion.

devanagari: चराचर गुरोर्विष्णोः ईश्वरस्याखिलात्मनः । धर्मत्राणाय साधूनां जन्म कर्मापनुत्तये ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: carācara gurorviṣṇoḥ īśvarasyākhilātmanaḥ dharmatrāṇāya sādhūnāṃ janma karmāpanuttaye
translation: Lord Viṣṇu is the Creator and Preceptor of the mobile and immobile creation. He is the Controller of the world and the inner Soul abiding in all beings. His advent (on. the earth) is for the protection of the path of righteousness and for wiping out all the Karmas of the righteous and to lead them to Final Emancipation from Saṃsāra.

devanagari: संभलग्राम मुख्यस्य ब्राह्मणस्य महात्मनः । भवने विष्णुयशसः कल्किः प्रादुर्भविष्यति ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: saṃbhalagrāma mukhyasya brāhmaṇasya mahātmanaḥ bhavane viṣṇuyaśasaḥ kalkiḥ prādurbhaviṣyati
translation: It is in the houses of Viṣṇuyaśas, the noble-souled Brāhmaṇa chief of the village called Śambhala[2], that Lord Kalki will be incarnated.
purport:
Footnote [2]:
A village near Moradabad in Rohilkhand, 80 miles to the cast of Delhi. It is ‘Sambalaka’ according to Ptolemy.—GDAMI, p. 176

devanagari: अश्वं आशुगमारुह्य देवदत्तं जगत्पतिः । असिनासाधुदमनं अष्टैश्वर्य गुणान्वितः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: aśvaṃ āśugamāruhya devadattaṃ jagatpatiḥ asināsādhudamanaṃ aṣṭaiśvarya guṇānvitaḥ
translation: The Lord of the Universe, endowed with eight Spiritual powers (like aṇimā, etc.) and excellences (like solemnity of vows or truthfulness) was unsurpassed in splendour and glory. Riding on a fleet horse called Devadatta, he would traverse over the whole earth on the horse; will massacre with his sword capable of subjugating the crores and crores of robbers and wicked miscreants who posed themselves as kings.

devanagari: विचरन् आशुना क्षौण्यां हयेनाप्रतिमद्युतिः । नृपलिङ्गच्छदो दस्यून् कोटिशो निहनिष्यति ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: vicaran āśunā kṣauṇyāṃ hayenāpratimadyutiḥ nṛpaliṅgacchado dasyūn koṭiśo nihaniṣyati
translation: The Lord of the Universe, endowed with eight Spiritual powers (like aṇimā, etc.) and excellences (like solemnity of vows or truthfulness) was unsurpassed in splendour and glory. Riding on a fleet horse called Devadatta, he would traverse over the whole earth on the horse; will massacre with his sword capable of subjugating the crores and crores of robbers and wicked miscreants who posed themselves as kings.

devanagari: अथ तेषां भविष्यन्ति मनांसि विशदानि वै । वासुदेवाङ्गरागाति पुण्यगंधानिलस्पृशाम् । पौरजानपदानां वै हतेष्वखिलदस्युषु ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: atha teṣāṃ bhaviṣyanti manāṃsi viśadāni vai vāsudevāṅgarāgāti puṇyagaṃdhānilaspṛśām paurajānapadānāṃ vai hateṣvakhiladasyuṣu

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]
[present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]
[locative plural from √ han class 1 verb], [locative plural from √ han class 2 verb]
[locative plural from √ han class 1 verb], [locative plural from √ han class 2 verb]

translation: (To indicate the beginning of the Golden Age—Kṛta Age) Thereupon, when the entire class of robbers, thieves and such enemies of the society is annihilated both in the town and in the country, the minds of the rural and urban population become pure by the touch of the wind wafting the sacred fragrance of pigments on the person of Lord Vasudeva.

devanagari: तेषां प्रजाविसर्गश्च स्थविष्ठः संभविष्यति । वासुदेवे भगवति सत्त्वमूर्तौ हृदि स्थिते ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: teṣāṃ prajāvisargaśca sthaviṣṭhaḥ saṃbhaviṣyati vāsudeve bhagavati sattvamūrtau hṛdi sthite

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ sthā class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ sthā class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ sthā class 1 verb], [locative single from √ sthā class 1 verb]
[nominative dual from √ sthā class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ sthā class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ sthā class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ sthā class 1 verb]

translation: When the glorious Lord Vasudeva, the very embodiment of strength, is enthroned in their heart, their progeny will naturally be extremely powerful.

devanagari: यदावतीर्णो भगवान् कल्किर्धर्मपतिर्हरिः । कृतं भविष्यति तदा प्रजासूतिश्च सात्त्विकी ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: yadāvatīrṇo bhagavān kalkirdharmapatirhariḥ kṛtaṃ bhaviṣyati tadā prajāsūtiśca sāttvikī

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

[nominative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation: When Lord Hari the Protector of righteousness, will descend on this earth as Kalki, there will be the advent of the Kṛta Age (Golden Age), and the children who will be born thereafter will be of pious (Sāttvika) disposition.

devanagari: यदा चन्द्रश्च सूर्यश्च तथा तिष्यबृहस्पती । एकराशौ समेष्यन्ति भविष्यति तदा कृतम् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: yadā candraśca sūryaśca tathā tiṣyabṛhaspatī ekarāśau sameṣyanti bhaviṣyati tadā kṛtam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ sūr class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ sūr class 10 verb]
[present middle first single], [imperative active second single]
[nominative plural from √ iṣ class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ iṣ class 4 verb], [accusative plural from √ iṣ class 4 verb]
[accusative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

[nominative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation: When rhe Moon, the Sun and the Jupiter are in conjunction in the same zodiacal house and the star Puṣya is in ascendance, the Kṛta age dawns.[3]
purport:
Footnote [3]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā: Here the simultaneous entry of these planets is indicated. Otherwise every twelfth year when the Jupiter is in the zodiacal sign of Cancer, there arc at least two or three New Moon days when this phenomenon occurs. Hence Simultaneous entry of these three planets is the sine qua non of Kṛta Age.

devanagari: येऽतीता वर्तमाना ये भविष्यन्ति च पार्थिवाः । ते ते उद्देशतः प्रोक्ता वंशीयाः सोमसूर्ययोः ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: ye te te uddeśataḥ proktā vaṃśīyāḥ somasūryayoḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ vṛt class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ vṛt class 1 verb]
[nominative plural from √ vṛt class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ vṛt class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ vṛt class 1 verb]

translation: All the kings belonging to the Lunar and Solar dynasties who have ruled in the past, or are reigning at present or will govern in the future, have been briefly described to you.

devanagari: आरभ्य भवतो जन्म यावत् नन्दाभिषेचनम् । एतद् वर्षसहस्रं तु शतं पञ्चदशोत्तरम् ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: ārabhya bhavato janma yāvat nandābhiṣecanam etad varṣasahasraṃ tu śataṃ pañcadaśottaram
translation: The period commencing from the birth of your worship to the coronation of Nanda would come to one thousand one hundred and fifteen years.[4]
purport:
Footnote [4]:
As Bhāvāratha Dīpikā notes this statement is ambiguous. But the author of the Bhāgavata Purāṇa is not much at fault, as we have not yet been able to fix tḥc date of the Mahābhārata War. Pargiter on the basis of various statements in Purāṇas has tentatively suggested 950 B.C. as the date of that war. But Jayaswal, A.C. Das and other Indian scholars favour 1500 B.C. Pusalkar in BVB’s Vedic Age (p. 273) concedes 1400 B.C. as the date of the Kuru- Pāṇḍava War. The discussion of the date of the Mahābhārata war is beyond the scope of this note. But I still believe in Ray Chaudhari’s evidence presented in his Political History of Ancient India from the Accession of Parīkṣit to the Extinction of the Gupta Dynasty, p. 9, 1923, and regard Parīkṣit as belonging to 900 B.C. This date has no relation with the composition of the present text of the Bhāgavata Purāṇa as shown in Intro, to Volume I of the Bhāgavata Purāṇa

devanagari: सप्तर्षीणां तु यौ पूर्वौ दृश्येते उदितौ दिवि । तयोस्तु मध्ये नक्षत्रं दृश्यते यत्समं निशि ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: saptarṣīṇāṃ tu yau pūrvau dṛśyete uditau divi tayostu madhye nakṣatraṃ dṛśyate yatsamaṃ niśi

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ vad class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ vad class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ vad class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Of the seven stars comprising the constellation the Great Bear, two of those asterisms come to view first., in the east (viz. Pulaha and Kratu). Midway between them in the north-western line, is noticed a star or a constellation (of the group of Aśvinī, Bharaṇī, etc.) at night. Those Heavenly Sages i.e. the stars Pulaha and Kratu remain associated with that intervening constellation for a period of one hundred human years. Those Sages (stars) are now associated with Maghā during your reign.

devanagari: तेनैव ऋषयो युक्ताः तिष्ठन्त्यब्दशतं नृणाम् । ते त्वदीये द्विजाः काले अधुना चाश्रिता मघाः ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: tenaiva ṛṣayo yuktāḥ tiṣṭhantyabdaśataṃ nṛṇām te tvadīye dvijāḥ kāle adhunā cāśritā maghāḥ

[nominative single from √ sthā class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ sthā class 1 verb]

translation: Of the seven stars comprising the constellation the Great Bear, two of those asterisms come to view first., in the east (viz. Pulaha and Kratu). Midway between them in the north-western line, is noticed a star or a constellation (of the group of Aśvinī, Bharaṇī, etc.) at night. Those Heavenly Sages i.e. the stars Pulaha and Kratu remain associated with that intervening constellation for a period of one hundred human years. Those Sages (stars) are now associated with Maghā during your reign.

devanagari: विष्णोर्भगवतो भानुः कृष्णाख्योऽसौ दिवं गतः । तदाविशत् कलिर्लोकं पापे यद् रमते जनः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: viṣṇorbhagavato bhānuḥ kṛṣṇākhyo tadāviśat kalirlokaṃ pāpe yad ramate janaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: When (the Maghās were in that position) the selfilluminating personality comprised of pure Sattva of Lord Viṣṇu who came to be known as Śrī Kṛṣṇa retired to his region, Kali immediately entered the world and people began to take delight in sinful ways.

devanagari: यावत् स पादपद्माभ्यां स्पृशनास्ते रमापतिः । तावत् कलिर्वै पृथिवीं पराक्रान्तुं न चाशकत् ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: yāvat sa pādapadmābhyāṃ spṛśanāste ramāpatiḥ tāvat kalirvai pṛthivīṃ parākrāntuṃ na cāśakat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ spṛś class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ spṛś class 6 verb]
[present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: While the Lord of the goddess Lakṣmī used to touch the earth with his lotus-feet, till then Kali did not dare to encroach upon the earth.

devanagari: यदा देवर्षयः सप्त मघासु विचरन्ति हि । तदा प्रवृत्तस्तु कलिः द्वादशाब्द शतात्मकः ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: yadā devarṣayaḥ sapta maghāsu vicaranti hi tadā pravṛttastu kaliḥ dvādaśābda śatātmakaḥ
translation: When those seven Divine Sages (Ursa Major) enter the constellation Maghā, there will be the advent of Kali lasting for twelve hundred celestial years (i.e. 432,000 human years).

devanagari: यदा मघाभ्यो यास्यन्ति पूर्वाषाढां महर्षयः । तदा नन्दात् प्रभृत्येष कलिर्वृद्धिं गमिष्यति ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: yadā maghābhyo yāsyanti pūrvāṣāḍhāṃ maharṣayaḥ tadā nandāt prabhṛtyeṣa kalirvṛddhiṃ gamiṣyati

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ yā class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ yā class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ yā class 2 verb]
[imperative active second single], [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: When these Great Sages (the asterisms, constituting the Great Bear) will proceed from Maghā to Pūrvāṣāḍhā, Kali will be more powerful since the ascension of king Mahāpadma Nanda.

devanagari: यस्मिन् कृष्णो दिवं यातः तस्मिन् एव तदाहनि । प्रतिपन्नं कलियुगं इति प्राहुः पुराविदः ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: yasmin kṛṣṇo divaṃ yātaḥ tasmin eva tadāhani pratipannaṃ kaliyugaṃ iti prāhuḥ purāvidaḥ
translation: Experts in ancient Lore have stated that on the very day—nay at that very hour—Lord Kṛṣṇa retired to his region, the Kali Age has set in.

devanagari: दिव्याब्दानां सहस्रान्ते चतुर्थे तु पुनः कृतम् । भविष्यति तदा नॄणां मन आत्मप्रकाशकम् ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: divyābdānāṃ sahasrānte caturthe tu punaḥ kṛtam bhaviṣyati tadā nṝṇāṃ mana ātmaprakāśakam

translation: At the termination of the period of one thousand divine years of the fourth i.e. Kali age, the Kṛta age will dawn again and the minds of men will get the revelation of the Self from within.

devanagari: इत्येष मानवो वंशो यथा सङ्ख्यायते भुवि । तथा विट्शूद्रविप्राणां तास्ता ज्ञेया युगे युगे ॥ ३५ ॥

translation: Just as the career or ups and downs in the history of Manu’s race has been described, in each age, the history of Vaiśyas, Śūdras and Brāhmaṇas in various ages should be understood to be similar to them (age after age).

devanagari: एतेषां नामलिङ्गानां पुरुषाणां महात्मनाम् । कथामात्रावशिष्टानां कीर्तिरेव स्थिता भुवि ॥ ३६ ॥

translation: It is only the names of the noble-souled persons described so far and of whom (neither kingdom nor race survives except) the legendary account enshrining them remains, (nothing but) their fame only survives, on the earth.

devanagari: देवापिः शान्तनोर्भ्राता मरुश्चेक्ष्वाकुवंशजः । कलापग्राम आसाते महायोगबलान्वितौ ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: devāpiḥ śāntanorbhrātā maruścekṣvākuvaṃśajaḥ kalāpagrāma āsāte mahāyogabalānvitau

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle third dual], [perfect middle third dual]

translation: Devāpi, the brother of Śantanu, and Maru, the descendant of the Ikṣvāku race—both of them possess very great Yogic powers and are still living in village called Kalāpa (Near Badarikāśrama in the Himalayas).

devanagari: ताविहैत्य कलेरन्ते वासुदेवानुशिक्षितौ । वर्णाश्रमयुतं धर्मं पूर्ववत् प्रथयिष्यतः ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: tāvihaitya kalerante vāsudevānuśikṣitau varṇāśramayutaṃ dharmaṃ pūrvavat prathayiṣyataḥ

translation: At the end of the Kali age, both of them, under the instructions and command of Lord Vāsudeva, will come forward and will promulgate and establish the ancient religious order with its institution of Varṇas (Classes of people) and Āśramas (Stages of life) once again as before.

devanagari: कृतं त्रेता द्वापरं च कलिश्चेति चतुर्युगम् । अनेन क्रमयोगेन भुवि प्राणिषु वर्तते ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: kṛtaṃ tretā dvāparaṃ ca kaliśceti caturyugam anena kramayogena bhuvi prāṇiṣu vartate

translation: This cycle of four Yugas (Ages) viz. Kṛta, Tretā, Dvāpara and Kali runs in this order on the earth for the sake of created beings.

devanagari: राजन् एते मया प्रोक्ता नरदेवास्तथापरे । भूमौ ममत्वं कृत्वान्ते हित्वेमां निधनं गताः ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: rājan ete mayā proktā naradevāstathāpare bhūmau mamatvaṃ kṛtvānte hitvemāṃ nidhanaṃ gatāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation: O king Parīkṣit, these kings described by me and many others (not mentioned by me) all claimed that the earth was theirs. But ultimately they had to leave it and passed away.

devanagari: कृमिविड् भस्मसंज्ञान्ते राजनाम्नोऽपि यस्य च । भूतध्रुक् तत्कृते स्वार्थं किं वेद निरयो यतः ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: kṛmiviḍ bhasmasaṃjñānte rājanāmno bhūtadhruk tatkṛte svārthaṃ kiṃ veda nirayo yataḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[accusative plural from √ i class 2 verb], [ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]
[ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: The physical body which (when alive) is designated as King is ultimately called worms (if buried or left uninterred and is turned into worms), or “Excrement” (if eaten by beasts or birds of prey and after digestion or non-digestion thrown out as faeces) or Ashes (if cremated). It is for this body that one perpetrates sins and commits violence against living beings. Does he not know that this sinful conduct leads to hell?

devanagari: कथं सेयमखण्डा भूः पूर्वैर्मे पुरुषैर्धृता । मत्पुत्रस्य च पौत्रस्य मत्पूर्वा वंशजस्य वा ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: kathaṃ seyamakhaṇḍā bhūḥ pūrvairme puruṣairdhṛtā matputrasya ca pautrasya matpūrvā vaṃśajasya vā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ sai class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ sai class 1 verb]
[accusative single from √ si class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ si class 9 verb]
[nominative single from √ si class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ si class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ si class 9 verb], [accusative single from √ si class 9 verb]

translation: How will this entire earth held under their own (undisputed sovereignty) by my forefathers and now held under my sceptre, pass on the same way to my son and grandson and his race?

devanagari: तेजोऽबन्नमयं कायं गृहीत्वाऽत्मतयाबुधाः । महीं ममतया चोभौ हित्वान्तेऽदर्शनं गताः ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: tejo mahīṃ mamatayā cobhau hitvānte
translation: Rulers who are unwise (indulge in such thoughts anxiously), look upon their physical body made up of heat, water and food as their Soul, and the earth as their personal property, ultimately quitting them both, sink into oblivion.

devanagari: ये ये भूपतयो राजन् भुंजते भुवमोजसा । कालेन ते कृताः सर्वे कथामात्राः कथासु च ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: ye ye bhūpatayo rājan bhuṃjate bhuvamojasā kālena te kṛtāḥ sarve kathāmātrāḥ kathāsu ca

translation:
O Parīkṣit, All those lords of the earth who enjoy the earth by dint of their personal power, have been reduced by Time to mere themes of stories when people narrate the past events.
